steps.title: Arrange terms by degree
steps.content:
We need to find $(r^3 + 5r^2 + 7) + (r^2 + 8r + 12)$. Organize both polynomials by degree:

$$r^3 + 5r^2 + 0r + 7$$
$$+ 0r^3 + r^2 + 8r + 12$$

steps.title: Combine like terms
steps.content:
Add coefficients of each degree:
  • $r^3$ terms: $1 + 0 = 1$, so $r^3$
  • $r^2$ terms: $5 + 1 = 6$, so $6r^2$
  • $r$ terms: $0 + 8 = 8$, so $8r$
  • Constants: $7 + 12 = 19$

The sum is $r^3 + 6r^2 + 8r + 19$. That matches choice D — D is correct.
